message:
docs(raptorq): update unit test matrix to reflect D9 catalog linkage progress

Update the Structured Replay/Log Field Coverage column for three module
families to reflect the newly added D9 catalog entries and structured
failure context in GF256 and systematic tests:

- Sender/Receiver builders: note edge-case unit replay refs are now
  cataloged; broader builder-path schema coverage still in progress
- Systematic parameter lookup: note RFC-equation/degree/seed-overhead
  unit tests now emit structured replay context and are catalog-linked
- GF256 primitives: note core SIMD/scalar and nibble-table checks now
  carry structured replay context and D9 catalog refs

Also update the structured failure logging status summary to reflect
that systematic/GF256 sentinels are now in place, and refine gap #2
to clarify which test families still lack schema-aligned context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ## Unit ↔ E2E Traceability
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ Required unit failure fields (for matrix governance):
 Status:
 
 - structured fields are fully present in deterministic E2E report flow
-- unit-level structured context is present for key edge-case success+failure paths; full suite-wide replay-id propagation remains **partial** pending D7/D9 integration
+- unit-level structured context is present for edge-case paths plus systematic/GF256 deterministic regression sentinels; full suite-wide replay-id propagation remains **partial** pending D7 coverage completion
 
 ## Replay Catalog (D9)
 
@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@ Profile tags represented in catalog entries:
 Open gaps identified during matrix pass:
 
 1. Unit suites do not yet consistently emit explicit `replay_ref` IDs on every failure path.
-2. Some pure GF256 and builder-path tests rely on assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
+2. Some builder and GF256/property-style tests still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
 3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for builder/systematic/GF256 families until schema-aligned failure context is universal.
